page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a base speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Cache resources include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 105 MB of L3 cache. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography flags like AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance divergence between single-core and multi-core workloads. The instance achieves average performance in single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU testing (1618.89) and Geekbench single-core evaluations (1713.0). Single-threaded operations like gzip compression (26.37 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (11.4 GB/sec) also fall into the average tier. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks are consistently weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This includes stress-ng multi-core performance (3315.66), Geekbench multi-core scores (3813.0), and Passmark CPU scores (6118.41). Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block speeds at 28.42 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 21.13 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-4 offers a dedicated hardware profile with a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but its resource density is constrained by weak multi-threaded scaling and low memory bandwidth. It is best utilized for workloads that rely on single-threaded CPU execution, basic static web serving (which achieved an average throughput of 6.68 GB/sec), and lightweight text processing. It can also handle small-scale LLM inference, processing 390.79 tokens per second on a 135M parameter model, though performance drops significantly on larger models. It is less suitable for database operations, software compilation, or memory-bound parallel processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 105 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This server does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1713.0 and steady single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are weak, placing in the bottom 25% of tested systems. Qualitatively, the server provides a dedicated hardware entry point but exhibits resource-density tradeoffs under parallel loads. It is best suited for static web serving, single-threaded applications, and lightweight text processing rather than intensive database operations or software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 is a general-purpose server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-4 features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering balanced single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server with balanced memory-to-CPU allocation, optimized for single-threaded tasks and static web serving without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Average single-core performance but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for static web serving, single-threaded tasks, and light text processing
